title: package.json is not exported
body:
We are using a tool that determines all versions of our main dependencies (needed for module federation) and had an issue with d3. As we are using a mono-repo with yarn workspaces, we are using the node.js resolving algorithm to get the right package.json and read from there the version number:
`require.resolve(`d3/package.json`).version`

However for d3 node throws an error:
`Error [ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED]: Package subpath './package.json' is not defined by "exports" in /path/to/node_modules/d3/package.json`

It seems like node recommends all packages to export the package.json as well for such use-cases:
https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/33460

Question is, is it intended that `d3` does not export the `package.json` in the `exports` property? Can this be changed and is a PR welcome for this?


To reproduce:
```
npm init -f
npm i d3
touch test.js
echo "require.resolve('d3/package.json')" > test.js
node test.js
```

Actual:
ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED

Should:
Would be nice if no error is thrown :partying_face:

author: DAmrGharieb

title: Apparent case of Chicken and Egg when updating nodes
body:
I'll preface with a disclaimer that I might be using the library wrong.  Still, it would be helpful to know what the expected answer to this problem is.  Roughly what I'm doing:

1. Have a `var simNodes` that contains the nodes used for the sims.
2. Have a `var simLinks` that contains the links used.
3. Remove a node (let's call it `node11`) from simNodes along with any simLinks entry that references it.
4. Call `simulation.nodes(simNodes)` to remove the node from the simulation.
5. Call `simulation.force("links").links(simLinks)` to remove the forces that were acting on that node.
6. Call alpha and restart to kick the graph into action.

After a bunch of debugging I discovered that the problem I'm running into is that the objects in `simLinks` have `source` and `target` defined as getters instead of just references.  When a force that acted on `node11` was evaluated by the simulation the getter  would return the entry in `simNodes` for `node11` ... which is fine if the node exists, but those links are getting evaluated after I removed the node and that's a problem.  I thought I had accidentally left a link somehow but couldn't find a source.  Much digging later I found this note:

> [#](https://github.com/d3/d3-force#simulation_nodes) simulation.nodes([nodes]) · [Source](https://github.com/d3/d3-force/blob/master/src/simulation.js)
> 
> If nodes is specified, sets the simulation’s nodes to the specified array of objects, initializing their positions and velocities if necessary, and then [re-initializes](https://github.com/d3/d3-force#force_initialize) any bound [forces](https://github.com/d3/d3-force#simulation_force); returns the simulation.

Re-initializes the forces?  Eh?  Further down, forceLink says...

> When the link force is [initialized](https://github.com/d3/d3-force#force_initialize) (or re-initialized, as when the nodes or links change), any link.source or link.target property which is not an object is replaced by an object reference to the corresponding node with the given identifier.

So, if I'm understanding all this correctly, updating either the nodes or links causes an evaluation of the source and target of all links, which in this case means step 4 triggers access of the links before I can update them in step 5.  If I flip those two steps, so I update the links before nodes, I'll hit the same issue when adding nodes instead.  If I have a change set that both adds and removes nodes I'd get the error regardless of which I tried to update first.

I know the obvious answer here is to not use a getter but I'd prefer to keep them if possible.  Another obvious answer would be to return a dummy object, but that feels event worse.  Looking at the code, though, it seems like the same issue exists when links are referenced by identifier like in the second quote...

https://github.com/d3/d3-force/blob/main/src/link.js#L58
```    for (i = 0, count = new Array(n); i < m; ++i) {
      link = links[i], link.index = i;
      if (typeof link.source !== "object") link.source = find(nodeById, link.source);
      if (typeof link.target !== "object") link.target = find(nodeById, link.target);
      count[link.source.index] = (count[link.source.index] || 0) + 1;
      count[link.target.index] = (count[link.target.index] || 0) + 1;
    }```

It seems to me that if initialize gets called with links where the source or target are identifiers, and that identifier refers to a node not in the sim, find throws an error and the call to links() fails.  Since the property is overwritten it shouldn't be an issue when removing nodes, no lookup is done in that case.  It would be a problem if you tried to add to the simulation though, specifically when adding the link before adding the node.

Thoughts?  Is there a sensible way to do this without making `.source` and `.target` be objects?  Is there supposed to be an order for updating the nodes and forces?

For the time being I'm using a work around of calling `simulation.force("links").links([])" before updating the node list in step 4, so as to change the node list while there's no links present, but that's not exactly optimal.

title: CI: Set minimum permissions on GitHub Workflow
body:
Hi!

I'd like to suggest the addition of top-level minimal permissions on your workflow, as it would harden your security agains supply-chain attacks.

I see that you have only one workflow, the [node.js.yml](https://github.com/d3/d3/blob/main/.github/workflows/node.js.yml), but it does not specify the permissions for its jobs, letting their privileges determined by GitHub's defaults. By defining minimal permissions you would be secured against erroneous or malicious actions from external jobs you call from your workflow. It's specially important for the case they get compromised, for example.

Setting minimum permissions for workflows is recommended by [GitHub itself](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/automatic-token-authentication) and also by other security tools, such as [Scorecards](https://github.com/ossf/scorecard) and [StepSecurity](https://github.com/step-security).

I'd be happy to raise a PR with the changes if you agree.

#### Context
I'm Diogo and I work on Google's Open Source Security Team([GOSST](https://github.com/diogoteles08#about-gosst-ghost)) suggesting and implementing security changes on critical open source projects :blush:

title: Zooming to a certain point instead of mouse position
body:
I'm using d3@7.3.0, and I would like to make a zoomable/pannable timeline with d3 zoom and a d3 timescale. My issue is that I want the timeline to zoom in and out of the center of the timeline as opposed to where my mouse position is. Here is my current behaviour: 
![chrome-capture-2023-2-15](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/97198784/225365816-d299afea-3b24-4a4b-a609-056aec4c1cee.gif)

And an example behaviour that I would like it to follow (note the zooming being on the center point of the timeline):
![AcsClient_XlkGMni9iu](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/97198784/225367780-57f6a287-5912-4068-9fe0-2511dc448fbf.gif)

I've noticed that older versions of d3 had the zoom.center() function that seemed to cater to this behaviour, ([an example that used it in a similar way to what I want](https://observablehq.com/d/48ff4a64c477cc79)) but it doesn't exist anymore. What would be the best way to implement this? Here is a general layout of my current code for reference as well (very simple of course):
```
const scale = d3
        .scaleTime()
        .domain([thirtyMinsAgo, now])
        .range([0, timelineWidth - 1])

const zoom = d3
        .zoom()
        .scaleExtent([0.0025, 250])
        .on('zoom', ({ transform }) => {
          scaleRef.current = transform.rescaleX(scale);
          scaleTimeline(scaleRef.current);
        })

svg.call(zoom);
```

title: Complex charting solution required
body:
I would like to build a report of the sample which I have attached. Can you please tell me if this is possible using D3.
In my report lots of charts are displayed. Like 1 for Heart Rate, Temperature, Respiration Rate and so on. I refer to this as a chart section below.

Some of the features that I require in my reports are mentioned below. Chart should be able to behave as a chart to display various chart types along with that it should also be able to display as a tabular view to display data in each cell. Some sections display charts like line chart or bar chart or combination of more than 2 chart types in a single chart or tabular format.
I have attached a PDF which has a sample type of charts that I require along with the below lists indicated in that chart for more clarity.

	1. The Chart section should be able to show in the form of a grid.
	2. In each grid cell I should be able to show a text - Tabular chart.
	3. Should be able to strikethrough a column completely with a red line
	4. Should be able to leave a vertical line space inside the chart based upon some logical conditions that we have (for ex, if the patient has a 24 hours of gap between the observations then we should show a vertical gap)
	5. Should be able to add a legend on the right side. Sometimes the legend should be mapped to a chart section (in such case legend should be aligned against the chart section row) , sometimes it can be common to all the chart sections. Here the chart section I refer to as heart rate, respiration rate and so on.
	6. I should be able to show multiple graph types in a single chart section - refer multiplegraphs-1 attachment
	7. In the chart section the ranges are shown as a separate label which needs to be aligned to the chart grid rows. Is this possible within the chart or we need a separate control which needs to be aligned to the chart rows.
	8. The chart section should not span across pages.
	9. Multiple Y-axis. Not sure if we will have multiple x-axis. But multiple y-axis.
	10. Does it support all the browsers and mobile devices like android and ios?
	11. Can we have an interactive chart?
	12. Does it have 3D support?
	13. Can we place an icon inside the chart grid cell?
	14. Can we show an overlay on the chart?
	15. Can we write a display a letter with an exponential like A to the power of 2
	16. To how much extent can we compress the chart to fit more pages and the font is still visible and looks nice
	17. Zooming option
	18. Panning
horizontal scrolling- this could be a separate chart altogether. please refer horizontal-scrolling-chart attachment
I have marked the numbers in the chart. Please look at the chart. And let me know if all these are possible or not. If possible, can you build a code pen project of the sample report which I have attached -ptn0445789-EWSReport-2 (1) . I can play around with that. May be one in client side with multiple pages to see the performance impact as well

I have attached 2 images and 2 pdf files. Please have a look at all of them. Mainly at the PDFs first

title: Add a Security-Policy
body:
Hi! I'm Diogo and I'm back (see #3636) hoping to offer a bit more help with security enhancements.

This time I'm here to suggest that you expose a way that users can report eventual vulnerabilities in a safe and efficient way. This is usually done through a Security Policy, which is a GitHub standard document (SECURITY.md) added on the root of the repo and that will be visible to the users in the "Security Tab", as you can see bellow:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22223372/225964433-be72a4a5-a57f-4b93-a138-6f2319e41c17.png)

Having a Security Policy is a recommendation from [Github](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/getting-started/securing-your-repository#setting-a-security-policy) itself, and from [Scorecard](https://github.com/ossf/scorecard/blob/main/docs/checks.md#security-policy) (being a security measure of medium priority).

Aiming to make this change easier, I'll take the liberty and submit a suggestion of a Security Policy as a PR. Please feel free to edit it directly or ask me for editions until it is in compliance with how d3/d3 would best handle vulnerability reports.

title: Add harmonic mean and geometric mean
body:
Harmonic mean is the sum of all reciprocals (which is one divided by a number), and the geometric mean is the product of numbers raised to one divided by number of items in a list. 
Harmonic mean = $\dfrac{n}{x_{1} + x_{2} + x_{3} + ... + x_{n}}$
For example, the harmonic mean of 2, 3, and 5 is 3/(1/2+1/3+1/5) = 3/(15/30+10/30+6/30) = 3/(31/30) = 3*30/31 = 90/31 = 2 28/30 = 2 14/15
Geometric mean =  $\sqrt[n]{{x_{1} \times x_{2} \times x_{3} \times ... \times x_{n}}}$

For example, the geometric mean of 2, 3, and 5 is (2*3*5)^(1/3)=30^(1/3)=3.1072

Sources:
https://byjus.com/maths/harmonic-mean/
https://www.scribbr.com/statistics/geometric-mean/

title: d3.min and d3.least don't always ignore nulls
body:
The documentation states, for `d3.min`, that "Unlike the built-in `Math.min` this method ignores `undefined`, `null` and `NaN` values", so I would expect that if I had an array:
```
array = ['5', '10', null, '2']
```
then `d3.min(array)` would return `'10'` (which is expected, since natural order is used), and `d3.min(array, d => +d)` would return `2`.

But no, `d3.min(array)` returns `0` (as expected, because `null` coerces to zero _before_ the accessor function is called, but the user that expects nulls to be ignored might not care to verify this). Although the issue is actually in the conversion, this is easy to overlook and can lead to bugs. 

The documentation for `d3.least` doesn't explicitly mention that it ignores null, but in this example, `d3.least(array, d => +d)` returns `null` (shouldn't it produce the same result as `d3.min` when using an accessor?)

  y.domain([0, d3.max(data, function(d) { return d3.max(keys, function(key) { return d[key]; }); })]).nice();

  g.append("g")
    .selectAll("g")
    .data(data)
    .enter().append("g")
      .attr("transform", function(d) { return "translate(" + x0(d.State) + ",0)"; })
    .selectAll("rect")
    .data(function(d) { return keys.map(function(key) { return {key: key, value: d[key]}; }); })
    .enter().append("rect")
      .attr("x", function(d) { return x1(d.key); })
      .attr("y", function(d) { return y(d.value); })
      .attr("width", x1.bandwidth())
      .attr("height", function(d) { return height - y(d.value); })
      .attr("fill", function(d) { return z(d.key); });

  g.append("g")
      .attr("class", "axis")
      .attr("transform", "translate(0," + height + ")")
      .call(d3.axisBottom(x0));

  g.append("g")
      .attr("class", "axis")
      .call(d3.axisLeft(y).ticks(null, "s"))
    .append("text")
      .attr("x", 2)
      .attr("y", y(y.ticks().pop()) + 0.5)
      .attr("dy", "0.32em")
      .attr("fill", "#000")
      .attr("font-weight", "bold")
      .attr("text-anchor", "start")
      .text("Population");

  var legend = g.append("g")
      .attr("font-family", "sans-serif")
      .attr("font-size", 10)
      .attr("text-anchor", "end")
    .selectAll("g")
    .data(keys.slice().reverse())
    .enter().append("g")
      .attr("transform", function(d, i) { return "translate(0," + i * 20 + ")"; });

  legend.append("rect")
      .attr("x", width - 19)
      .attr("width", 19)
      .attr("height", 19)
      .attr("fill", z);

  legend.append("text")
      .attr("x", width - 24)
      .attr("y", 9.5)
      .attr("dy", "0.32em")
      .text(function(d) { return d; });
</script>
</html>

```

title: Documentation website (observablehq.com) TLS (mis?)configuration
body:
I'm not sure if this is the correct place to raise this issue, but I'm not sure of a better one...

The main documentation website (https://d3js.org/) appears to be configurated correctly, but most of the examples there link to the Observable website (https://observablehq.com/), which is configured such that only TLS 1.3, but not TLS 1.2 traffic can connect. 
This means that the Observable website is inaccessible from 1-2% of all browsers on the internet. But also, it's inaccessible through some widely-used corporate firewalls (e.g. ZScaler), that don't support TLS 1.3 yet. 

Given that this website isn't just for d3js documentation but is also the front door for the commercial Observable framework, not allowing access to corporations (and potential customers) that use TLS 1.2 firewalls would seem to be problematic. 

I would think that configuring https://observablehq.com/ to support both TLS 1.3 AND 1.2 traffic would improve accessibility to the site. This is why 99.9% of websites (per https://www.ssllabs.com/ssl-pulse/) still support TLS 1.2 traffic.

title: non-ascii chars in minified bundle
body:
While building a new JavaScript benchmark for [JetStream](https://github.com/WebKit/JetStream/pull/124) we've observed that the minified d3 bundle contains non-latin1 characters (specifically `µ` and `−`).

This has the negative side-effect that the browser engines parse the whole d3 bundle as slower 2-byte string (as opposed to the more efficient 1-byte string representation).

For slightly faster parsing it would make sense to escape these strings with something similar like this [webpack plugin](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dapplets/unicode-escape-webpack-plugin).

title: Consider Adopting NPM Trusted Publishing
body:
> ## Overview
> Recent [supply chain attacks on npm](https://github.blog/security/supply-chain-security/our-plan-for-a-more-secure-npm-supply-chain/) have highlighted the need for stronger package publishing security. The September 2025 Shai-Hulud worm compromised 500+ packages through stolen maintainer tokens, showing the risks of token-based publishing.
> 
> Trusted publishing helps by eliminating long-lived tokens that can be stolen or accidentally exposed; generating automatic provenance provides cryptographic proof of where/how packages are built; and is an industry standard adopted by `PyPI`, `RubyGems`, `crates.io`, `NuGet`, etc...
> 
> `NPM` is [planning to deprecate legacy tokens](https://github.blog/security/supply-chain-security/our-plan-for-a-more-secure-npm-supply-chain/#h-npm-s-roadmap-for-hardening-package-publication) and make trusted publishing the preferred method.
> 
> If assistance is welcome, please let me know and I can assist and/or get 
> further assistance as needed.
>
> ## Reference
> References:
> 
> * [NPM trusted publishing documentation](https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers)
> * [Announcement blog post](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-07-31-npm-trusted-publishing-with-oidc-is-generally-available/)
> * [Provenance statements guide](https://docs.npmjs.com/generating-provenance-statements)

title: type assertion syntax error prevents building this module with node 22+
body:
type assertion using `assert` are deprecated in node [v.22](https://github.com/nodejs/node/releases/tag/v22.0.0);  support for `with` was introduced in node [v18.20](https://github.com/nodejs/node/releases/tag/v18.20.0) and [v20.10](https://github.com/nodejs/node/releases/tag/v20.10.0).

We can adopt the change and change this in `rollup.config.js`:

```diff
-import meta from "./package.json" assert {type: "json"};
+import meta from "./package.json" with {type: "json"};
```

and then the `engines` entry in `package.json` should be `>=18.20` (minimal), or `>=20` (current LTS).

Alternatively, update `engines` to request `"node": ">=12 <20"`.

(I suppose this is also true for other D3 packages.)

We do not accept PRs for this issue, it's just a decision we have to make.

title: Unchecked Input for loop condition vulnerability in the minified bundle
body:
I'm using D3 7.9.0 in which Checkmarx flags a line in the minified d3 bundle. The vulnerability is [Unchecked input for loop condition](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/606.html) and this is the line in question in function Gd(t, n) { 
`....
6195. for (var e, r, i, o, a, u = new Qd(t), c = [u]; e = c.pop();) if
((i = n(e.data)) && (a = (i = Array.from(i)).length)) for (e.children =
i, o = a - 1; o >= 0; --o) c.push(r = i[o] = new Qd(i[o])), r.parent =
e, r.depth = e.depth + 1;
`

The report says:

"Method Gd at line 6195 of d3.min.js gets user input from element data
. This element’s value flows through the code without being validated, and is eventually used in
a loop condition in Gd at line 6195 of d3.min.js. This constitutes an
Unchecked Input for Loop Condition."

Please consider mitigating this vulnerability.

|         suffix = (symbol === "$" ? currencySuffix : /[%p]/.test(type) ? percent : "") + (options?.suffix !== undefined ? options.suffix : "");
| 
 
It appears that the issue is related to optional chaining (?.) syntax, which is not being transpiled correctly in our current build setup.

Kindly check and help resolve this issue at the earliest.

Thanks,
Vivek MN

title: feat: introduce d3-geometry module for automatic topology, flow, and system-to-geometry visualization
body:
## Summary

This PR introduces a new experimental module, `d3-geometry`, which enables
automatic transformation of system-level data (graphs, processes, event flows)
into dynamic geometric representations.

D3 currently provides powerful primitives for mapping explicit data to visual
properties, but lacks support for extracting and visualizing implicit structure
such as topology, cycles, and temporal flow.

This module adds a higher-level abstraction layer to represent hidden geometry
behind complex systems.

---

## Motivation

Modern applications increasingly rely on understanding complex systems:
- distributed architectures
- real-time data pipelines
- AI/ML model structures
- network and security graphs

These systems contain implicit geometry (relationships, clusters, feedback loops)
that are not directly visible through traditional charting approaches.

This PR aims to extend D3's capabilities from "data visualization"
to "structure and system visualization".

---

## Key Features

### 1. Geometry Mapping API
Provides a declarative interface to map input data into geometric structures:

```js
geometry()
  .input(data)
  .mapping("graph")
  .layout("force")
@D3

title: Typescript issue referencing transition
body:
I have multiple cases where I create a transition on an element, and then refer to it in a sub-element, synchronising the transitions. With TypeScript `strictFunctionTypes` set to true, it appears impossible to type check this correctly.

The simples example I can think of is

```
let svgElement: SVGSVGElement = ...;
const s = select(svgElement);
const t1 = s.transition();
const t2 = transition(t1);
```

This is obviously artificial, but demonstrates the typing error from a more complex case. Compiling results in the error:

```
error TS2769: No overload matches this call.
  Overload 1 of 2, '(name?: string | undefined): Transition<BaseType, unknown, null, undefined>', gave the following error.
    Argument of type 'Transition<SVGSVGElement, unknown, null, undefined>' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.
  Overload 2 of 2, '(transition: Transition<BaseType, any, BaseType, any>): Transition<BaseType, unknown, null, undefined>', gave the following error.
    Argument of type 'Transition<SVGSVGElement, unknown, null, undefined>' is not assignable to parameter of type 'Transition<BaseType, any, BaseType, any>'.
      The types returned by 'selection().data(...).on(...)' are incompatible between these types.
        Type '((this: SVGSVGElement, event: any, d: any) => void) | undefined' is not assignable to type '((this: BaseType, event: any, d: any) => void) | undefined'.
          Type '(this: SVGSVGElement, event: any, d: any) => void' is not assignable to type '(this: BaseType, event: any, d: any) => void'.
            The 'this' types of each signature are incompatible.
              Type 'BaseType' is not assignable to type 'SVGSVGElement'.
                Type 'null' is not assignable to type 'SVGSVGElement'.
```

It seems clear that, although you would expect SVGSVGElement to be assignable to BaseType, the issue is the opposite, because a function type is involved (from the event handler I guess). Any thoughts on resolving this?
author: sgjohnston

title: docs:build in CI is not exercising the same prebuild path as deploy
body:
While looking at the docs build, I noticed that the test and deploy workflows are not feeding `docs:build` the same inputs.

`test.yml` runs `yarn docs:build`, but `deploy.yml` first checks out `d3/d3.github.com` into `build/d3.github.com`. `prebuild.sh` assumes that directory exists and starts copying files from it immediately.

In a fresh clone, running `./prebuild.sh` without that extra checkout fails right away with missing file errors from `./build/d3.github.com/...`.

That made me wonder whether CI is actually validating the same docs build path that deploy uses, or whether these are meant to be different builds.

Repro:
1. Fresh clone of this repo.
2. Run `./prebuild.sh` or `yarn docs:build` without checking out `d3/d3.github.com` into `build/d3.github.com`.
3. The copy step fails immediately because the expected files are not there.

Files involved:
- `.github/workflows/test.yml`
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
- `prebuild.sh`

Maybe the fix is to make the test workflow check out `d3/d3.github.com` too, or to make `prebuild.sh` fail early with a clearer message if that checkout is required, or to split the build paths if they are intentionally different.

I might be missing some intended setup here, but at the moment the test and deploy docs builds look out of sync.
